sprint: Unified Layer Ordering — Object Strip Becomes a Real Reorder Control
task: Direct follow-up to the Sticker Drag/Hit-Test Z-Order fix: the user asked to fix the layer-order buttons for real, then proposed the Object Strip itself become the reorder control ('can object strip be used as layering order? if we reorder objects there the layering reorder itself?'). Investigated and confirmed today's layering was three completely disjoint mechanisms with no shared key: Scene blueprint elements ordered by a numeric zIndex (SceneEngine.adjustZIndex), Stickers ordered by plain array position (SceneEngine.bringStickerForward/sendStickerBackward/bringStickerToFront/sendStickerToBack), and World-owned Layer Pack objects ordered purely by containership scope. renderer/slideRenderer.js's render() assembled the paint/hit-test array as a fixed concatenation -- Scene elements then Stickers, always in that order -- so a sticker could never be reordered relative to a Frame/Decoration blueprint element on any page role, and with one sticker on a page every Order button was a correct-but-useless no-op. Fixed by introducing one additive override, slide.metadata.layerOrder (absent for every page today, zero behaviour change until first use), and a real resolution pipeline: _naturalStoryOrder/_resolveStoryOrder/getReorderableIds in renderer/slideRenderer.js compute, respectively, the pre-override natural order (byte-identical to the old fixed sequence), the order after applying any layerOrder override (unlisted objects appended stably), and the public accessor both Card Designer and Object Strip read. Card Designer's three separate Order-button blocks (Sticker/Frame/Decoration) were collapsed onto one shared _reorderSelected(edge, commitFn) helper so they can never disagree with the Object Strip. Object Strip (js/objectStrip.js) gained real HTML5 drag-and-drop (_wireReorderDrag/_performReorder) on every card whose object is in the current reorderable set -- dragging a card now changes real paint and hit-test order. World-owned Layer Pack objects were deliberately scoped OUT of this round's reorderable group, a disclosed engineering judgment call made mid-implementation: unifying them would require deferred/two-pass drawing changes to the Frame/Panel rendering pipeline itself, risking regressions for existing themes (including the Hosted-by-Scene background mechanism from an earlier sprint) that depend on the current fixed slide/frame/holder/element/overlay draw-timing. Verified via a new Playwright suite (natural order matches the old fixed sequence; a Sticker Front-button click moves a lone sticker to the top of the unified order; three Decoration Forward-button clicks move a Scene element past overlapping stickers -- the core cross-mechanism proof; real dragstart/dragover/drop DragEvents dispatched on actual Object Strip cards change the persisted layerOrder; a synthetic locked World-owned object is confirmed absent from getReorderableIds) plus full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
